title: Rootless: Permission Denied Starting Container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[x]: # **kind bug**


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull jacobalberty/unifi:stable`

2. `podman run -d --net=host --name=unifi-sdn-controller -e TZ='America/New_York' -v /var/opt/unifi:/unifi:z jacobalberty/unifi:stable unifi`

**Describe the results you received:**

I got a permission error:

```
[alc@atomic ~]$ podman run -d --net=host --name=unifi-sdn-controller -e TZ='America/New_York' -v /opt/unifi:/unifi:z jacobalberty/unifi:stable unifi
error mounting image volumes: error creating directory "/var/home/alc/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/2c0e37c5ccc482eb8a31e71043cda77f294cd90b86e48dd5b4950b2ec05eb812/userdata/volumes/var/run/unifi" for volume "/var/run/unifi" in container %!q(func() string=0xf3ed90): mkdir /var/home/alc/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/1fe8ef1a906e5e6ca090b38f8fe087b7673dca11d9da698d52504448022059d8/var/run/unifi: permission denied
```

Also, things that shouldn't be owned by root, seem to be owned by root:

```
[alc@atomic ~]$ ls -l /var/home/alc/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/1fe8ef1a906e5e6ca090b38f8fe087b7673dca11d9da698d52504448022059d8/var/run/
total 36
-rw-------.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:39 agetty.reload
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 auditd.pid
drwxrwx---.  2 ceph    ceph      40 Oct  8 15:39 ceph
drwxr-x---.  2 chrony  chrony    60 Oct  8 15:39 chrony
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 chronyd.pid
drwx------.  2 root    root      80 Oct  8 15:39 cloud-init
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 console
drwx------.  3 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:44 containers
drwxr-xr-x.  7 root    root     140 Oct  8 15:39 container-storage-setup
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 criu
drwx------.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 cryptsetup
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:39 dbus
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 dhclient-enp1s0.pid
prw-------.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:39 dmeventd-client
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 dmeventd.pid
prw-------.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:39 dmeventd-server
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 faillock
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root     100 Oct  8 15:39 fsck
drwxrwxr-x.  2 gluster gluster   40 Oct  8 15:39 gluster
-rw-------.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 gssproxy.pid
srw-rw-rw-.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:39 gssproxy.sock
drwxr-xr-x.  4 root    root     120 Oct  8 15:39 initramfs
drwxr-x--x.  5 root    root     140 Oct  8 15:44 libpod
drwxr-xr-x.  5 root    root     100 Oct  8 15:39 lock
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 log
drwx------.  2 root    root      80 Oct  8 15:39 lvm
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 lvmetad.pid
drwx--x---.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:37 mdadm
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 media

drwxrwxr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 netreport
drwxr-xr-x.  3 root    root     100 Oct  8 15:39 NetworkManager
-rw-r-----.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:39 ostree-booted
drwx------.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 podman
drwx------.  2 rpc     rpc       40 Oct  8 15:39 rpcbind
srw-rw-rw-.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:37 rpcbind.sock
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       0 Oct  8 15:37 rpc.statd.pid
drwx------.  3 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:48 runc
drwxrwxr-x.  2 root    screen    40 Oct  8 15:39 screen
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 sepermit
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:39 setrans
-rw-------.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 sm-notify.pid
-rw-r--r--.  1 root    root       4 Oct  8 15:39 sshd.pid
drwx--x--x.  3 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:39 sudo
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:39 sysconfig
drwxr-xr-x. 21 root    root     500 Oct  8 15:41 systemd
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      60 Oct  8 15:37 tmpfiles.d
drwxr-xr-x.  7 root    root     160 Oct  8 15:40 udev
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root      40 Oct  8 15:48 unifi
drwxr-xr-x.  4 root    root      80 Oct  8 15:48 user
-rw-rw-r--.  1 root    utmp    1536 Oct  8 15:41 utmp
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container to just work as it had before.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.9.1
Go Version:    go1.10.4
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.9.1-3.gitaba58d1.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 1d75f1d54e9e123a97e7f505e38df7ab7192cfa7-dirty'
  MemFree: 120201216
  MemTotal: 2077765632
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-51.dev.gitfdd8055.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 2214051840
  SwapTotal: 2214588416
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: atomic.ccbp.online
  kernel: 4.18.9-200.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 22m 48.6s
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /var/home/alc/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 28 atomic host.

comments.body: @giuseppe PTAL - I don't think we have the permissions to chown this to root as rootless, so I'm wondering if this is potentially something about AH and where we put our paths?

so the issue is not really with rootless containers, but they helped to expose it as running without root privileges.
`~/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/3bdad44e99a6f8749ee6962c17d21e2682ec716ebb918f7bc611b302d2895f6d/var/run` is an absolute symlink to `/run`.  We need to resolve it in the scope of the chroot, otherwise we end up using host absolute paths (such as `/run`).  The `ls -l` output you posted is really coming from the host `/run` that is why all the files are owned by root.  The fix is to resolve the symlinks in the chroot scope.  Buildah has already a function for doing it, but it is not public, I've opened a PR there as well and I'll rebase the podman PR on top of that once it is merged:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1076
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1609
